evals.name: Fastify API scaffold completeness
evals.prompt: Scaffold a Fastify 4 REST API for a task management system with resources: tasks and users. JWT auth, Zod validation, Drizzle ORM + PostgreSQL, OpenAPI spec, integration tests.
evals.expected_output: Complete scaffold with config.ts, app.ts, routes, handlers, services, Zod schemas, Drizzle schema, and integration tests

assertions.text: Output includes src/config.ts with Zod env validation
Output includes src/app.ts with Fastify plugin registrations
Output includes Zod schemas for request bodies and path params
Output includes Drizzle schema with uuid primary keys and timestamps
Output includes JWT authentication middleware or preHandler
Output includes rate limiting configuration
Output includes global error handler that never exposes stack traces
Output includes GET /health endpoint
Output includes integration tests using Fastify inject()
Output includes multi-stage Dockerfile
Output does NOT use express (Fastify was specified)

evals.name: Security audit of insecure API
evals.prompt: Audit this API: no validation on POST /users body, JWT_SECRET='secret', CORS origin='*' with credentials:true, error handler sends err.stack, no rate limit on /auth/login.
evals.expected_output: Security report with CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM severity findings and specific fixes

assertions.text: Output flags missing body validation as CRITICAL or HIGH
Output flags JWT_SECRET='secret' as CRITICAL
Output flags CORS origin=* with credentials as HIGH or CRITICAL
Output flags err.stack exposure as HIGH
Output flags missing login rate limit as HIGH
Output provides specific code fix for each finding

evals.name: OpenAPI spec generation
evals.prompt: Write an OpenAPI 3.1 spec for a users CRUD API with JWT auth. Include all CRUD endpoints, consistent error schema, pagination on list, JWT bearer security scheme.
evals.expected_output: Complete openapi.yaml with paths, components/schemas, security scheme, all response codes

assertions.text: Output starts with openapi: '3.1.0'
Output includes components/securitySchemes with bearerAuth
Output includes all CRUD paths: GET /, POST /, GET /:id, PATCH /:id, DELETE /:id
Output documents 400 and 401 responses for protected write endpoints
Output uses $ref for schema references (not inline duplication)
Output includes pagination params on list endpoint

evals.name: Handler architecture validation
evals.prompt: Review this handler: it contains a direct Drizzle query inside the route handler function body, business logic (discount calculation), and console.log for debugging. Flag the issues.
evals.expected_output: Architecture review identifying handler anti-patterns with specific refactoring guidance

assertions.text: Output flags DB query in handler as anti-pattern (should be in service)
Output flags business logic in handler (should be in service)
Output flags console.log (should use pino logger)
Output provides refactored version separating handler, service, and repository

evals.name: Integration test setup for route coverage
evals.prompt: Write integration tests for GET /api/v1/products (list with pagination), POST /api/v1/products (requires admin JWT), and GET /api/v1/products/:id (returns 404 if not found). Use Fastify inject().
evals.expected_output: Test file with 3 describe blocks, each with happy path, validation error, and auth/not-found tests

assertions.text: Output uses Fastify inject() not real HTTP requests
Output tests 400 for invalid pagination params
Output tests 401 for missing JWT on POST
Output tests 404 for non-existent product ID
Output uses beforeAll to build app once and afterAll to close
Output checks response body shape, not just status code
